name: feat-04-dashboard-widgets
initialPrompt: The dashboard is a static page and nobody looks at it. I want a widget-based dashboard where users can add, remove, and rearrange widgets. Widgets needed for v1: key metrics (revenue, signups, active users), a recent activity feed, a quick-links panel, and a chart showing weekly trends. Each user should have their own layout that persists. Also add a widget catalog so we can ship new widgets without a full deploy.

blocks.text: Turn the static dashboard into a personalised, widget-based workspace
successCriteria: new widgets deployable without code release

blocks.question: Where do we persist per-user widget layouts?
blocks.rationale: Allows versioning and sharing layouts in future.

blocks.title: Build key metrics widget (revenue, signups, active users)
Build widget catalog for adding widgets to the dashboard
acceptance: user can add any widget with one click
new widget appears at top of grid

blocks.description: Many simultaneous widgets each polling APIs may overload the server
blocks.mitigation: Batch widget data requests into a single dashboard API endpoint

blocks.question: Should some widgets be role-gated (e.g. revenue visible only to admins)?

blocks.text: Turn the static dashboard into a personalised, widget-based workspace
successCriteria: new widgets deployable without code release

blocks.title: Build batched dashboard data API endpoint
acceptance: single request returns data for all active widgets
response cached for 30 s per user

blocks.question: Where do we persist per-user widget layouts?
blocks.rationale: Allows versioning and sharing layouts in future.

acceptance: line chart renders revenue and signups over 7 days

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same risk (widget polling overload) but updated after batched API task added; id changed to reflect revision. AC-13 re-anchoring fallback must map risk-render-perf to risk-render-perf-v2.

decoy.note: Genuinely-new block. Shares surface tokens 'widget', 'dashboard', and 'metrics/trends' with task-metrics-widget. A comment on task-metrics-widget must re-attach to task-metrics-widget (id preserved), NOT this new chart widget.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-dashboard-api inserted second (positional shift for all); task-trends-chart-widget added; risk-render-perf id changed. Naive positional scheme loses alignment after API task insertion. Denominator is 7.
